author: @nicksgarage8295
text: the only issue at 4:39 i see is that the drop outs for the wheel are facing upwards, which means if the bolts is lose, the wheel and you hit a bump, the wheel will pop out while your doing 50mph. They should face downwards instead!!! Welds look great. good project ngl. Stick or mig/fcaw ?

author: @endadalton
text: Luke you have done a great job. Super job.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you very much! :)

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
Not sure why I am not getting notifications for new videos. Sorry for the late viewing Luke. 
Couldn't see the drawing you said was there in 1:1 ratio, would have been more visible with a black marker instead of pencil which I'm assuming you used. That brake could be dangerous as the metal is hitting on the incoming rotation of the tyre. I would use traditional bike calipers that use friction on the wheel rim.
Excellent video all told, your enthusiasm is fantastic, great work mate.

author: @paulbrouyere1735
text: Hey Luke, nice project. Only one remark: a drawing of black on a red surface is never good on camera, too little contrast. You’re doing well, please continue.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I wish I used a sharpie, it would have looked better on the cam. Thanks mate! :)

author: @andytrewin
text: Well done Luke.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Cheers mate! :)

author: @danielade9694
text: You did a wonderful job on it

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you mate! :)

author: @danielade9694
text: Learning by practice is the best way to find out if a design is good or needs to be improved! And to be willing to say that I need to change this cuz I can do better than this is a good start to solving problems that you find in the design that didn't meet the expectations that you expected of it! Good work keep up improving your skills

author: @honeyflowbakery
text: Mr Luke, thanks for taking the time to capture, edit, publish your work. Watching, thinking helps fight the mindclamp of lockdown. Am excited to see more.

author: @ryanlebeck259
text: You certainly have improved your ability to present as well as your maker skills. I love the colors of your scooter as well, that is a sharp little kicker you've got there mate! Well done.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you very much mate! :)

author: @another3997
text: Great stuff. Best put the front brakes on the fork, just in case you really need to stop in a hurry.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Yes that is a great idea, The mounts that are there already were too high. I'm thinking about welding some new rods in the correct place, so I can attach them with ease. Thanks mate! :)

author: @robertpitt8418
text: That scooter looks brilliant - thanks for sharing your projects sir - have fun : )

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey

author: @undernetjack
text: Good job.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey

author: @jamescunliffe9872
text: Excellent! 10x better than mk1=) just one thing I'd mention, is the stem bolts cap the original or a washer? Without what looks like a top hat with a hole, loose description, a plain washer can slip and make you impersonate superman! Don't ask how I know!;) good job nice joints!

author: @charlesdickens6706
text: ......good , carry on ; all designs go through stages..mk1 then mk2 then mk3.......at some stage you might choose to give bronze brazing a go , doesn't need to be oxyacetylene, oxypropane is fine and carbon arc flame also works .

author: @markhodgson2348
text: Luke you need to make a Scooter

author: @PaddyNinja
text: Yeah bud, the welding skills are there and the riding skills are definitely there.  You should copy the one wheel m8 and get Mr Smith to test it out on a hill. lol. Cheers m8 Well done.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahaha that would be an interesting video LOL. Cheers mate! :)

author: @hempev
text: Philosophise with a hammer, testing idols to see if they ring true.

author: @NorthernKitty
text: Impressive work!!!  "Remaking" things is a good habit to get into... it's the best way to really cement your skills and "know-how", making them second nature and instinctive.  😊 We won't learn from past mistakes if we don't follow them up with improvements, as you've done here. 😊  Really great job!!!  🥰

replies.author: @RocketSteve

author: @MrAnderson4509
text: Luke did you leave the openings for the rear axil drop outs on top?

replies.author: @synchro-dentally1965
replies.text: It does seem risky to have them oriented like that especially if it will be used on rough terrain.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I did fit some locking tags, so the wheel should not move when riding.

author: @buckhinschwey
text: Just put the old bicycle brakes on, the front studs are there and wouldn’t be too hard to weld some on for the rear

author: @martinX74

replies.author: @dolanbaker
replies.text: Yes I can see that, He's going to need to fit some locking tags or reverse them otherwise he will lose the wheel while riding.

author: @DiyEcoProjects
text:
Hi Luke, i like this chunky build 4.50 nice one LOl. Also your videos are improving mate, very clear, well done :)

Can i ask, do you think that the front bar where the footplate is, is it going to clip curbs when you go over them? you might end up arse over tit. If thats the case, just thinking perhaps cut the size down on the upright that holds the front wheel. Anyway, love it - looks great.

author: @fallknight5405
text: Did you come across an issue with your other design if so I understand how you feel I come across issues all the time when I'm building something an search online to see if anyone's building something similar

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: It is not that I came across and issue as such, It is the fact how I didn't like it that much, it felt very sturdy and rode very nicely. But my building and planning skills have improved so I thought I could do a better job rebuilding one lol. Cheers mate! :)

author: @anunnakielohim2727
text: well done mate, now you just need to go over and "borrow" that cordless drill that Robert just made a video about, connect the motor to the rear wheel, put the battery under the foot board and connect the trigger on the handle bars near the hand grip and away you go!!!!!

replies.author: @CraigDavies.
